result:
## `#ifdef USE_JEMALLOC` (4 sites) — **memory allocator subsystem**

| File:Line | What's gated | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `src/zmalloc.c:637` | `zfree_with_size()` body — uses `je_sdallocx()` (sized free) vs. falling back to plain `zfree()` | Core allocator wrapper — jemalloc-specific fast free path with size hint |
| `src/debug.c:349` | `mallctl_int()` function definition (wraps `je_mallctl`) | Exposes jemalloc's `mallctl` tuning interface for ints |
| `src/debug.c:460` | `MALLCTL <key> [<val>]` / `MALLCTL-STR <key> [<val>]` lines in `DEBUG HELP` output | Help text only shown when jemalloc-specific subcommands exist |
| `src/debug.c:1104` | `DEBUG MALLCTL` / `DEBUG MALLCTL-STR` command dispatch branches inside `debugCommand()` | Routes the DEBUG command to `mallctl_int`/`mallctl_string` |

All four exist to let Redis call into jemalloc's private `mallctl()` tuning API (via `je_mallctl`/`je_sdallocx`), which is only linked when Redis is built against jemalloc — this is squarely the **memory-allocator subsystem** (`zmalloc.c` is the allocator abstraction layer; the `debug.c` sites are its jemalloc-only DEBUG-command surface).

## `#ifdef HAVE_BACKTRACE` (10 sites) — **crash/debug reporting subsystem**

All 10 are in `src/debug.c`, none elsewhere in the tree:

| Line | Enclosing context | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| 35 | top of file, `#include <execinfo.h>` / `<ucontext.h>` | Only pull in backtrace headers if platform supports `backtrace()` |
| 1260 | `_serverAssert()` | Calls `logStackTrace()` before producing the crash report on an assertion failure |
| 1381 | `_serverPanic()` | Same — logs stack trace before crash report on a panic |
| 1408 | opens block containing `getAndSetMcontextEip()` | Reads/sets instruction pointer from a `ucontext_t`, needed to decode crash context — whole helper only compiled when backtraces are available |
| 2111 | opens block with `BACKTRACE_MAX_SIZE` / `logStackTrace()` machinery | Core stack-trace-printing implementation (uses `backtrace()`/`backtrace_symbols()`) |
| 2564 | `sigsegvHandler()` | Reads faulting EIP via `getAndSetMcontextEip`, logs "Crashed running the instruction at: %p" |
| 2598 | `sigsegvHandler()` (same fn, later) | Calls `dumpCodeAroundEIP(eip)` to disassemble around the crash site |
| 2752 | `sigalrmSignalHandler()` | Declares `ucontext_t *uc` (else stubs it out) — used by the watchdog/SIGALRM stack-dump feature |
| 2766 | `sigalrmSignalHandler()` (same fn) | Calls `logStackTrace(...)`; `#else` branch logs "no support for backtrace()" |
| 2818 | opens the `__linux__` "Stacktrace Utils" section (thread-signal helpers used by the crash/watchdog stack dumper) | Linux-specific per-thread stack-trace collection helpers |

All 10 gate code that only makes sense when the C library provides `backtrace()`/`backtrace_symbols()` (glibc-style); they implement Redis's SIGSEGV/assert/panic/watchdog **crash-and-debug reporting** path (`sigsegvHandler`, `sigalrmSignalHandler`, `logStackTrace`, `dumpCodeAroundEIP`, EIP extraction, per-thread stack dumping). This is a completely separate subsystem from the jemalloc one above, despite living in the same `debug.c` file.

### Cross-check
`grep -rn "#ifdef USE_JEMALLOC\|#ifdef HAVE_BACKTRACE" .` (whole tree, `.git` excluded) returns exactly these 14 lines — 4 in `zmalloc.c`/`debug.c` for jemalloc, 10 all in `debug.c` for backtrace. No other files or build-system (`Makefile`) gating was found for these two flags — no changes made to the repo.
